steps.title: Recognize the sentence expresses a timeless characteristic
steps.content: The sentence is explaining what a zinc atom is—a defining property that is always true, not something that happened at a particular time.

steps.title: Apply the rule for stating universal truths
steps.content:
Simple present tense “has” correctly expresses characteristics that are always true.

The past tense options (had, has had) would incorrectly suggest this property only existed in the past, and present progressive (is having) would incorrectly suggest an ongoing action.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
